title: psilochenia occidentalis subsp. pumila
intro: Psilochenia occidentalis subsp. pumila, also known as a dwarf form of the Western yarrow plant, is a perennial herb native to the western regions of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing fever. The health benefits of Psilochenia occidentalis subsp. pumila include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce menstrual cramps, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, allowing for a range of uses in traditional medicine.
